source_reviews.notes: The official Census NAICS page is accessible and is used only to define the exact six-digit industry boundary, not a finalized score input.
The report supports the 68% planned increase, 8% regular strategic use, readiness barriers, automatable back-office tasks, durable producer functions, and customer preference for agent involvement.
The release supports independent-agency placement of 62% of total U.S. P&C written premium and 87.7% of commercial-lines written premium in 2025.

The NAIC page supports the requirement that people selling, soliciting, or negotiating insurance be licensed producers; the packet correctly avoids treating licensing as an exclusive channel franchise.
The disclosure supports fixed-percentage base commissions on new and renewal premium and incentive compensation tied to production, retention, growth, and profitability.
The article supports the 40% over-60 result among more than 450 principals and owners and documents ownership-transition and talent-pipeline challenges.
The report supports 695 announced 2025 deals, 73% PE/hybrid share, and the seven named buyers with 22 to 69 deals; the broader North American distribution scope is retained as a caveat.
The article supports approximately 12x public-broker trading multiples and roughly 14x private-platform base purchase prices, with near-19x values including earnouts.
The report supports 11.22x average all-firm and 14.02x platform-firm 2024 upfront base purchase prices and clearly defines the mixed roll-in, stand-alone, and platform population.

The registered legacy path was rate-limited during direct fetch, but the migrated official Big I page is accessible and supports ownership-change expectations, e-signature adoption, and direct-bill commission-statement adoption.

findings.explanation: The deterministic target-count bridge applies a 23.57% Damodaran Insurance (General) EBITDA margin to insurance agencies and brokerages, a cross-segment proxy that is transparently labeled ESTIMATE but is not agency-specific.
findings.materiality: A different agency-brokerage margin could change which SUSB size classes enter the $1-10M EBITDA band and therefore move B, but this does not overturn the M-factor-floor kill thesis and the proxy is disclosed rather than contradictory.
findings.remediation: On refresh, use an agency-brokerage margin benchmark or publish sensitivity of n_band and B to plausible agency margins.
findings.publication_caveat: dataset_inputs.n_band: The deterministic EBITDA-band count applies a 23.57% Damodaran Insurance (General) margin to insurance agencies and brokerages; although labeled ESTIMATE and transparently derived, this cross-segment margin proxy can move the buyability factor.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 52421 occupation mix includes MGAs, wholesale brokers and delegated-authority work as well as retail agencies; target books can differ materially.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions describe technically replaceable tasks at mature deployment, not current adoption or immediate headcount reduction.
inputs.pi_dist: Carrier commission schedules and contingent income are controlled upstream and can change; fee-based large-commercial accounts expose cost more directly.
inputs.pi_moat: Commercial-heavy agencies have more moat than personal-lines and standardized small-commercial books; licensing is not treated as an exclusive channel franchise.
inputs.t50_years: The survey measures agency-reported usage rather than verified labor substitution, and target sophistication varies sharply by size.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: National Big I member survey; regular strategic use is a stricter definition than any AI use and does not prove headcount removal.
inputs.historical_analogs: E-signature is a simpler, lower-liability tool than AI-assisted policy advice or autonomous workflow execution.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: More than 450 principals and owners were surveyed; it is not a Census estimate and may not match the $1–10M EBITDA cohort.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: A documented channel-wide challenge does not mean every target lacks an internal successor.
inputs.active_consolidators: North American announced-deal data covers insurance distribution broadly and includes transactions outside exact NAICS 524210 or the target EBITDA band.
inputs.buy_mult: MarshBerry mixes roll-ins, stand-alone firms and platforms and skews larger than many $1–10M EBITDA targets; earnouts and contingent consideration are excluded.

inputs.exit_mult: Exit requires genuine platform scale, organic growth, retention and infrastructure; a collection of small books may still price as a roll-in, and 2026 public comparables had fallen toward 12x.
inputs.pricing_structure: The Hanover is one carrier example and does not establish a universal commission percentage.

dataset_inputs.n_band: The deterministic EBITDA-band count applies a 23.57% Damodaran Insurance (General) margin to insurance agencies and brokerages; although labeled ESTIMATE and transparently derived, this cross-segment margin proxy can move the buyability factor.
summary: Mechanics, identity, routing, digests, deterministic dataset injection, arithmetic, fresh finalization, and memo rendering all pass. All ten registered sources were reopened or recovered at the official migrated page and support their attributed claims; S1 is not score-driving. The thesis and factor directions remain useful without material evidence defects or thesis-changing capture/terminal double counting, but publication must retain 13 authored caveats plus the validator caveat on the deterministic n_band margin proxy.
